title: Determination of Diphtheria Toxin in Bacterial Cultures by Enzyme Immunoassay
author: Vyacheslav G. Melnikov
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: An immunoassay based on monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) has been developed. A pair of mAbs specific to the fragment B of DT was selected, which makes it possible to detect DT in a sandwich ELISA with a detection limit of DT less than 1 ng/mL. Sandwich ELISA was used to analyze 218 liquid culture supernatants of high-, low- and non-toxigenic strains of various corynebacteria. The results of ELISA were in good agreement with the results of PCR and the Elek test. The diagnostic sensitivity of the assay was approximately 99%, and specificity was 100%.
steps_raw: An immunoassay based on monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) has been developed. A pair of mAbs specific to the fragment B of DT was selected, which makes it possible to detect DT in a sandwich ELISA with a detection limit of DT less than 1 ng/mL. The results of ELISA were in good agreement with the results of PCR and the Elek test. The diagnostic sensitivity of the assay was approximately 99%, and specificity was 100%.
keywords: Corynebacterium diphtheriae, Corynebacterium ulcerans, monoclonal antibodies, ELISA
